toolUseResult.originalFile:
slug: x016-baseline-2b-implementer
title: X016 Phase 2b — first implementer-driven trial
codex: nexus

description: |
  Phase 2b — first end-to-end trial that actually drives the
  implementer. Plugin set extended with animator + loom +
  claude-code so Spider can summon a real anima session.
  Spider's plugin-default `mandate → default` fallback handles
  template lookup with no explicit mapping required (verified
  in spider.ts:1389 — the fallback fires when no kit claims
  `mandate`, which is the case without astrolabe installed).

  guild.json givens layer in:
    - loom.roles.artificer with clerk:* tools:* (mirrors vibers)
    - animator.sessionProvider = 'claude-code'
    - spider.variables { role, buildCommand, testCommand } —
      build/test commands package-scoped via pnpm --filter to
      keep the implementer out of `pnpm -r` purgatory

  Brief retargeted at packages/framework/core (the smallest,
  zero-dep framework package) — see briefs/baseline-task-core.md.

  waitForTerminal=true with a 15-min cap. The expected wallclock
  is well under that for a healthy implementer session; the cap
  exists to bound a runaway. Cost expectation: ~$0.30/trial,
  budget for 1-2 debug rounds on first attempt.

  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY flows through to the test guild's daemon
  via process.env inheritance — `lab.daemon-setup`'s execFile
  call does not pass `env`, so the spawned `nsg start` inherits
  the lab-host's environment.

frameworkVersion: '0.1.292'

fixtures:
  - id: codex
    engineId: lab.codex-setup
    givens:
      # Same nexus-monorepo codex as phase 2a. Pinned to local
      # HEAD; local-bare flow tolerates unpushed commits.
      upstreamRepo: /workspace/nexus
      baseSha: 3c307a20a7afc33df96c87c1a2d694edfb951c05

  - id: test-guild
    engineId: lab.guild-setup
    dependsOn: [codex]
    givens:
      # Phase-2a's seven plugins + animator/loom/claude-code so
      # the `implement` engine can actually summon. No astrolabe
      # — Spider's plugin-default mandate→default fallback fires
      # automatically without it.
      plugins:
        - { name: '@shardworks/stacks-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/tools-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/codexes-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/clerk-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/spider-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/clockworks-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/fabricator-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/animator-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/loom-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
        - { name: '@shardworks/claude-code-apparatus', version: '0.1.292' }
      # Deep-merged into the test guild's guild.json so the
      # implementer wiring is in place when the daemon boots.
      config:
        loom:
          roles:
            artificer:
              permissions:
                - 'clerk:*'
                - 'tools:*'
        animator:
          sessionProvider: 'claude-code'
        spider:

            testCommand: 'pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test'

content.text: Filing a click for the underlying spider bug so we don't lose track of it.

input.command: nsg click open --parent c-monew2rg --goal "Spider 0.1.292's apparatus.start() calls g.apparatus('animator') directly but only declares requires=['stacks','clerk','fabricator']. This is a latent topo-sort ordering bug: it works in vibers (animator at position 0, spider at position 14) but fails any guild that declares spider before animator in guild.json plugins. Discovered during X016 phase-2b first trial (w-monn5qa9 failed) — error chain was 'apparatus(\"animator\") is not available' → spider start() throw → daemon crawl loop wedged with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading find)'. Worked around in the X016 manifest by ordering [animator, loom, claude-code] before [spider, clockworks]. Fix options: (1) add 'animator' to spider's requires, (2) switch to tryApparatus<AnimatorApi>('animator') with null guard since animator-paused is a kit-time block-type checker that does its own tryApparatus already, (3) defer the animator binding from start() to first-use lazy resolution. Option 1 is simplest and matches the actual coupling. Same bug pattern likely exists in other apparatuses: any start() that calls g.apparatus(X) without X in requires is a latent ordering bomb." 2>&1 | tail -10

content.content:
{
  "id": "w-monnjfo1-94f433021db1",
  "type": "mandate",
  "phase": "open",
  "title": "Add a multiplySafely utility to nexus-core",
  "body": "# Add a multiplySafely utility to nexus-core

Add a TypeScript function `multiplySafely(a: number, b: number):
number | null` that returns `null` when the multiplication would
overflow `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`, and otherwise returns
`a * b`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Function lives in
  `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.ts` (create the
  file and the `util/` directory if they don't exist).
- Function exported from `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.ts`.
- One unit test file at
  `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.test.ts` covering:
  a typical successful multiplication, an overflow case, and
  the boundary case where the result is exactly
  `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`.
- Tests pass under
  `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test`.
- Build passes under
  `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core build`.

## Context

This is a small bounded task chosen for the Laboratory
apparatus's first real implementer-driven trial run (X016 phase
2b). The trial codex is the nexus framework monorepo, so the
file targets the smallest, dependency-light package
(`@shardworks/nexus-core`) and the build/test commands are
package-scoped via pnpm filters to keep the implementer out of
the recursive `pnpm -r` path.

The brief is small enough to complete quickly in an implementer
session and large enough to produce a real animator session
record + at least one codex commit. Phase 2c will extend this
into the actual orientation-suppression A/B (baseline vs
strong-prompt brief variants).

## Notes for the implementer

- The package's existing tests use Node's built-in test runner
  (`node --test`) — see `id.test.ts` and `pid-helpers.test.ts`
  in the same `src/` directory for the test style.
- The package has zero runtime dependencies; do not add any.
- No need to update `index.ts` exports unless you want
  `multiplySafely` available as part of the public SDK surface
  — the brief only requires the function be exported from its
  own file.
",
  "codex": "x016-baseline-2b-implementer-373f8f51",
  "createdAt": "2026-05-02T01:17:26.161Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-02T01:17:26.162Z",
  "classification": "active",

  `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.test.ts` covering:
  a typical successful multiplication, an overflow case, and
  the boundary case where the result is exactly
  `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`.
- Tests pass under
  `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test`.
- Build passes under
  `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core build`.

## Context

This is a small bounded task chosen for the Laboratory
apparatus's first real implementer-driven trial run (X016 phase
2b). The trial codex is the nexus framework monorepo, so the
file targets the smallest, dependency-light package
(`@shardworks/nexus-core`) and the build/test commands are
package-scoped via pnpm filters to keep the implementer out of
the recursive `pnpm -r` path.

The brief is small enough to complete quickly in an implementer
session and large enough to produce a real animator session
record + at least one codex commit. Phase 2c will extend this
into the actual orientation-suppression A/B (baseline vs
strong-prompt brief variants).

## Notes for the implementer

- The package's existing tests use Node's built-in test runner
  (`node --test`) — see `id.test.ts` and `pid-helpers.test.ts`
  in the same `src/` directory for the test style.
- The package has zero runtime dependencies; do not add any.
- No need to update `index.ts` exports unless you want
  `multiplySafely` available as part of the public SDK surface
  — the brief only requires the function be exported from its
  own file.

- session: 7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37
  date: 2026-05-02
  item: "X016 phase 2b — first attempt (w-monn5qa9) and second attempt (w-monnj0vq) both failed; surfaced and fixed two real bugs. (1) Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb: spider's apparatus.start() calls g.apparatus('animator') but only declares requires=['stacks','clerk','fabricator']. Topo sort starts spider before animator unless animator appears first in the guild.json plugins array. Vibers (animator at position 0, spider at 14) hides this; the X016 test guild had spider before animator and failed loudly with 'apparatus(\"animator\") is not available'. Worked around in baseline-execution-2b.yaml by reordering plugins so [animator, loom, claude-code] precede [spider, clockworks]. Filed click c-monn8wfk under c-monew2rg with three fix options (preferred: add 'animator' to spider's requires) and noted same pattern likely exists in other apparatuses. (2) Real bug in lab.commission-post-xguild's poll loop: it called `nsg writ-show` (no such top-level command in 0.1.292 CLI) instead of `nsg writ show` (sub-subcommand). Earlier phases all ran with waitForTerminal=false and never exercised this path. Fixed in scenario-xguild.ts. 228/228 tests still passing. Trial about to be re-posted with both fixes in place."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

input.command:
git add packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.test.ts experiments/X016-orientation-suppression/manifests/baseline-execution-2b.yaml experiments/data/coco-log.yaml && GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Coco GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=coco@nexus.local git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
laboratory: fix writ-show typo + work around spider ordering bug for X016 2b

Two bugs surfaced by the first phase-2b trial attempt (w-monn5qa9):

1. lab.commission-post-xguild called `nsg writ-show` in its poll
   loop, but the published 0.1.292 CLI only has `nsg writ show`
   (sub-subcommand). Earlier phases ran with waitForTerminal=false
   and never exercised the poll path. Fixed by splitting the args
   to `['writ', 'show', ...]` and adding a context comment.

2. Spider 0.1.292's apparatus.start() calls g.apparatus('animator')
   but declares requires=['stacks','clerk','fabricator']. The topo
   sort starts spider before animator unless animator appears
   first in the plugin list. Vibers hides this (animator pos 0,
   spider pos 14); the X016 test guild's natural plugin order
   triggered it. Worked around in baseline-execution-2b.yaml by
   ordering [animator, loom, claude-code] before [spider, clockworks]
   with a comment pointing at the underlying bug click.

Underlying spider bug filed as c-monn8wfk under c-monew2rg for a
proper fix (preferred: add 'animator' to spider.requires; same
pattern likely exists across other apparatuses' start() methods).

228/228 laboratory tests still passing.

Session: 7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37
EOF
)"

[
  {
    "id": "rig-monnob7z-2f65d565",

    "status": "running",
    "engines": [

        "givensSpec": {
          "writ": {
            "id": "w-monno7rk-8dd1697d514a",
            "type": "mandate",
            "phase": "open",
            "title": "Add a multiplySafely utility to nexus-core",
            "body": "# Add a multiplySafely utility to nexus-core

Add a TypeScript function `multiplySafely(a: number, b: number):
number | null` that returns `null` when the multiplication would
overflow `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`, and otherwise returns
`a * b`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Function lives in
  `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.ts` (create the
  file and the `util/` directory if they don't exist).
- Function exported from `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.ts`.
- One unit test file at
  `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.test.ts` covering:
  a typical successful multiplication, an overflow case, and
  the boundary case where the result is exactly
  `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`.
- Tests pass under
  `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test`.
- Build passes under
  `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core build`.

## Context

This is a small bounded task chosen for the Laboratory
apparatus's first real implementer-driven trial run (X016 phase
2b). The trial codex is the nexus framework monorepo, so the
file targets the smallest, dependency-light package
(`@shardworks/nexus-core`) and the build/test commands are
package-scoped via pnpm filters to keep the implementer out of
the recursive `pnpm -r` path.

The brief is small enough to complete quickly in an implementer
session and large enough to produce a real animator session
record + at least one codex commit. Phase 2c will extend this
into the actual orientation-suppression A/B (baseline vs
strong-prompt brief variants).

## Notes for the implementer

- The package's existing tests use Node's built-in test runner
  (`node --test`) — see `id.test.ts` and `pid-helpers.test.ts`
  in the same `src/` directory for the test style.
- The package has zero runtime dependencies; do not add any.
- No need to update `index.ts` exports unless you want
  `multiplySafely` available as part of the public SDK surface
  — the brief only requires the function be exported from its
  own file.

# Experiments Index

Maintained by Coco. Update when experiments are created, activated, or closed.

## Active

| # | Name | Research Question |
|---|---|---|
| [X006](X006-guild-metaphor/spec.md) | The Guild Metaphor | Does the guild metaphor improve agent performance and patron experience compared to plain instructions? |
| [X008](X008-patrons-hands/spec.md) | The Patron's Hands | How does the bootstrap period (Coco doing implementation) shape patron expectations when autonomous agents come online? |
| [X010](X010-staged-sessions/spec.md) | Staged Sessions | What is the relationship between session length, cost, and output quality for autonomous commissions? Phase 1 (cost curve) complete at original scale; Apr 29 addendum sharpens H4 into H4a/H4b/H4c sub-hypotheses (cost model + handoff thresholds) and adds H5 (inventory pure-read bloat). |
| [X011](X011-context-debt/spec.md) | Context Debt | How much context window is consumed by tool output never referenced again, and can we reduce it? Activated 2026-04-29 with the read-utilization instrument as its first artifact. |
| [X016](X016-orientation-suppression/spec.md) | Orientation Suppression | When a fresh implementer session enters mid-flow, how many turns elapse before it produces productive work? Does an imperative anti-orientation directive in the brief reduce that turn count to <5? Phase 1 (apparatus validation) authored 2026-05-01 — first real-world Laboratory apparatus trial. |

## Ready (approved, not yet started)

| # | Name | Research Question |
|---|---|---|
| [X003](X003-commission-prompt-tuning/spec.md) | Commission Prompt Tuning | How do pre-commission instructions affect agent performance on identical tasks? |
| [X004](X004-iteration-context/spec.md) | Iteration Context | How does context about prior sessions affect quality and safety when an agent iterates on its own work? |
| [X009](X009-metaphor-as-instruction/spec.md) | Metaphor as Implicit Instruction | Does the guild metaphor carry actual behavioral weight, or is it decorative? |

## Draft (not yet approved)

| # | Name | Research Question |
|---|---|---|
| [X005](X005-greenfield-assembly/spec.md) | Greenfield Assembly | What if agents never iterate on existing code — only build net-new? |
| [X012](X012-agent-oriented-code/spec.md) | Agent-Oriented Code | Do conventional clean code principles still apply when agents are the primary authors? |
| [X014](X014-technical-spec-quality/spec.md) | Technical Spec Quality | Do technically detailed specs outperform mountain-quality specs — and does it depend on complexity? |
| [X015](X015-spec-detail-model-substitute/spec.md) | Spec Detail as Model Substitute | Can planning-pipeline specs reduce the model capability threshold, enabling Sonnet-class models to match Opus outcomes? |

  - Wallclock: ~16s end-to-end
  - Extracted artifacts: `artifacts/2026-05-02-baseline-2a-daemon-smoke/`
  - Codex pinned to nexus framework repo (`/workspace/nexus`) at SHA
    `3c307a20a7af` — local HEAD, not yet pushed (local-bare flow

    npx-bootstrapped guild, real npm-installed plugins, real `nsg
    start` daemon process, and real `nsg stop` shutdown. Phase 2b
    (implementer config + waitForTerminal=true) is unblocked.

- 2026-05-02: **Trial 2b (first implementer-driven trial) completed.**
  First end-to-end trial that drives a real implementer session
  through the full draft → implement → review → revise → seal pipeline
  inside a Laboratory test guild. Apparatus end-to-end-validated.
  - Trial id: `w-monnnuqw-c998a09d0e9f`
  - Rig: `rig-monnny9h-786e0be8` (16 engines, all completed in 1 attempt)
  - Archive: `lar-monnr5zx-7868fa26482d`
  - Wallclock: 2m 31s end-to-end (01:20:56 → 01:23:27 UTC)
  - Extracted artifacts: `artifacts/2026-05-02-baseline-2b-implementer/`
  - Plugin set extended to animator + loom + claude-code on top of
    phase-2a's seven. No astrolabe — verified that Spider's plugin-
    default `mandate → default` fallback (spider.ts:1389) fires
    automatically without an explicit mapping when no kit claims
    `mandate`.
  - guild.json deep-merged with `loom.roles.artificer` (clerk:* tools:*,
    mirroring vibers), `animator.sessionProvider = claude-code`, and
    `spider.variables` (role/buildCommand/testCommand) — build/test
    commands package-scoped via `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core`
    to avoid recursive-pnpm overhead.
  - **Three real animator sessions captured.** All three completed
    successfully (exitCode 0):
    - `implement` (artificer): 102.8s wallclock, $0.207, tokens
      15in / 5527out / 245k cache-read / 13k cache-write.
    - `review` (reviewer): 11.4s, $0.045, 3in / 380out / 11.5k cache-
      read / 8.8k cache-write. No rejection — minor-or-no revise needed.
    - `revise` (artificer): 8.1s, $0.026, 4in / 324out / 24.7k cache-
      read / 3.2k cache-write.
  - **Total session cost: $0.278.** Rig-level cost reported as
    $0.2782 (matches; no lab-host overhead beyond per-engine
    invocation).
  - **Codex commit captured.** 1 commit, 1562 bytes of diff:
    `feat(nexus-core): add multiplySafely utility` at SHA
    `7b984d611010` adding `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.ts`
    + `numeric.test.ts`. Function shape matches the brief precisely
    (overflow guard via `Math.abs(product) > Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`).
  - **Two real bugs caught and fixed in the same session:**
    1. Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb (filed as `c-monn8wfk` under
       `c-monew2rg`): spider.start() calls `g.apparatus('animator')`
       but only declares requires=[stacks,clerk,fabricator]. Worked
       around in the manifest by ordering [animator,loom,claude-code]
       before [spider,clockworks] in the plugin list.
    2. `lab.commission-post-xguild`'s poll loop called the non-
       existent `nsg writ-show` command (should be `nsg writ show`).
       Earlier phases all ran with waitForTerminal=false and never
       exercised this path. Fixed in scenario-xguild.ts.
  - Apparatus pipeline now end-to-end-validated against a live sonnet
    implementer + reviewer + revise + seal. Phase 2c (baseline vs
    strong-prompt A/B) is unblocked.
  - **Cost expectation reconciled.** Pre-trial budget was ~$0.30 per
    trial; actual cost was $0.28 with no debug rounds (the two bugs
    were caught at the apparatus level, not the implementer level —
    no API calls wasted on retries). Phase 2c with N runs per variant
    will scale linearly.

  Open follow-up captured but not yet filed:
  - `lab.probe-git-range`'s commits-manifest.yaml reports
    `filesChanged: 0, insertions: 0, deletions: 0` per commit even
    when the patch clearly contains content (1562 bytes of new file
    in this trial). Diff-stat extraction in the probe is broken;
    cosmetic but worth fixing before phase 2c so we have proper
    summary stats.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# X016 — Orientation Suppression

**Status:** Draft, Trial 1 (apparatus validation) authored 2026-05-01.

**Parent click:** `c-mok4qh81` — Idea #17 from the cost-optimization
landscape.

## Research question

When a fresh implementer session enters mid-flow (i.e., after a
handoff from a prior session), how many turns elapse before it
produces productive work? Does an imperative anti-orientation
directive in the brief reduce that turn count to <5?

## Background

The X010 H1 piece-session experiment (per-task implementer
checkpointing) failed because each fresh session paid an
"orientation tax": re-read the spec, re-grep the codebase, re-state
the plan, re-validate prior commits before producing any new output.
Total turn count expanded 3.5×, and the savings from cache-read
accumulation were swamped.

For idea #15 (checkpoint-and-fresh-session implement architecture)
to win, total turn count post-handoff must stay under ~1.3×
monolithic, which means each post-handoff session must produce
productive work in <5 turns. This experiment measures whether an
imperative directive in the implementer's prompt suppresses the
orientation tax.

## Hypothesis

**H1.** Adding an imperative directive ("Begin work immediately. Do
not summarize the task before starting. Make your first turn a code
change.") to the implementer's brief reduces median turns-to-first-
productive-edit from N (baseline) to fewer than 5.

## Variants

| variant | description |
|---|---|
| baseline | implementer brief carries no orientation-suppression directive |
| strong-prompt | brief carries the imperative directive above |

## Apparatus

This is the first real-world trial running through the **Laboratory
apparatus** (`packages/laboratory/`). Trial manifests live under
`manifests/`; archived data lives in vibers' `lab-trial-*` books;
extracted directories under `artifacts/<trial-slug>/`.

## Design notes

### Phase 1 — apparatus validation (this trial)

The first run validates the apparatus pipeline end-to-end without
attempting to execute a full implementer session. The test guild
posts a commission via `lab.commission-post-xguild` with
`waitForTerminal: false` — the writ lands but no rig is driven to
completion.

**Why phase 1 doesn't measure orientation behavior**: a full
implementer rig requires the test guild's Spider crawl loop to be
running, which currently means a clockworks daemon. The Laboratory
does not yet manage daemon lifecycle for test guilds; adding that is
a follow-up. Phase 1 instead validates:

- Codex setup (npx-bootstrapped local-bare flow)
- Test-guild bootstrap via `npx -p @shardworks/nexus@<v> nsg init`
- Plugin install loop via `<testGuild>/node_modules/.bin/nsg`
- Commission post lands (writ in `clerk/writs`)
- Probes capture: `lab.probe-stacks-dump` against the test guild's
  books, `lab.probe-trial-context` for rig metadata,
  `lab.probe-git-range` for codex commits (will be empty since no
  rig sealed)
- Archive engine writes `lab-trial-archives` row
- Teardown safety check passes (archive row exists)
- `nsg lab trial-extract` materializes captured data

### Phase 2 — full execution (future)

Once daemon lifecycle is added to `lab.guild-setup`, the manifest
can switch to `waitForTerminal: true` and the implementer rig will
actually run. At that point:

- baseline + strong-prompt variants both run as separate trials
- `lab.probe-stacks-dump` captures `animator/sessions` rows
  (turn counts, cost, durations) for each variant
- `lab.probe-git-range` captures the implementer's commits to
  measure productive-output timing
- Comparative analysis happens sanctum-side via
  `nsg lab trial-export-book <trialId> --book animator/sessions`
  piped into a DuckDB query

## Trial-shape constraints

- **Codex base SHA**: pinned to a specific sanctum SHA at trial
  authoring time (locks the test guild against a stable code state).
- **Framework version**: pinned to a published `@shardworks/nexus`
  version (currently `0.1.292`).
- **Plugin set**: vibers' standard set minus optional plugins
  (lattice/lattice-discord/oculus/parlour/sentinel are not
  installed in the test guild).

## References

- `docs/archive/deprecated-docs/experimental-infrastructure-setup-and-artifacts.md`
  — predecessor design that the apparatus replaces.
- `packages/laboratory/README.md` — apparatus authoring guide.
- Click `c-mok4nke6` — cost-optimization landscape (parent of #17).
- Click `c-mok4qh81` — idea #17, this experiment's parent.
- Click `c-modxxtu6` — idea #15, the architecture this binds to.

  - Archive: `lar-mondkc1h-ed27ba9f0587`
  - Wallclock: ~30s end-to-end
  - Extracted artifacts: `artifacts/2026-05-01-baseline-validation/`
  - Two real bugs caught and fixed in the same session:
    - `lab.probe-stacks-dump` used CommonJS `require('node:fs')` in
      an ESM module — replaced with proper imports.
    - `lab.archive`'s graft didn't pass `${writ}` through; the
      Spider's `resolveGivens` does substitute it for graft givens
      (just like static template), so `buildArchiveGraft` now emits
      `writ: '${writ}'` alongside the trial-context injection.
  - Apparatus pipeline validated end-to-end: npx-bootstrapped test
    guild, per-plugin install loop via test guild's local nsg,

    (1 writ in `clerk/writs`, 0 codex commits as expected),

  - Captured probe summaries make sense: `frameworkVersion: '0.1.292'`

    `frameworkVersion: '0.0.0'` on probe-trial-context's top-level

input.command: nsg click create --parent-id c-monew2rg --created-session-id 7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37 --goal "lab.probe-git-range writes commits-manifest.yaml with filesChanged/insertions/deletions all set to 0 even when the captured patch clearly has content. Surfaced in X016 phase 2b extract: 1 commit with 1562 bytes of diff (numeric.ts + numeric.test.ts both new files, ~30 lines total) reports {filesChanged: 0, insertions: 0, deletions: 0}. Probe is likely either (a) parsing `git log --numstat` output and missing the count line, (b) running git in the wrong cwd so the diff-stat returns empty, or (c) writing the manifest before computing stats. Worth a 30-min triage; cosmetic for the apparatus but distorts summary stats for phase 2c (baseline vs strong-prompt A/B will compare diff sizes across runs, and broken diff-stats break that comparison). Tight scope, no API cost." 2>&1 | tail -3

(2) Real bug in lab.commission-post-xguild's poll loop: it called `nsg writ-show` (no such top-level command in 0.1.292 CLI) instead of `nsg writ show` (sub-subcommand). content.text:
Phase 2b is in the books. Three commits this session:

1. `ce302ef4` — phase-2b brief + manifest authored
2. `77c473b7` — two real bugs fixed mid-trial (spider ordering bomb + `writ-show` typo)
3. `6666dbee` — first successful implementer-driven trial captured + artifacts

## Trial summary

| metric | value |
|---|---|
| trial id | `w-monnnuqw-c998a09d0e9f` |
| wallclock | 2 min 31 s |
| total cost | $0.278 (matched the $0.30 budget — no debug-round overhead because both bugs caught at the apparatus layer, not the implementer) |
| sessions | 3 (implement 102.8s/$0.207, review 11.4s/$0.045, revise 8.1s/$0.026) |
| codex commit | `feat(nexus-core): add multiplySafely utility` — 1562 bytes diff, 2 new files exactly matching the brief |
| engines | 16/16 completed in 1 attempt each |

## What got fixed along the way

- **Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb** (filed as `c-monn8wfk`) — `spider.start()` calls `g.apparatus('animator')` but only declares `requires: ['stacks','clerk','fabricator']`. Vibers hides this (animator at position 0); the test guild's natural plugin order tripped it. Worked around by re-ordering `[animator, loom, claude-code]` before `[spider, clockworks]` in the manifest with a comment pointing at the bug click.
- **`writ-show` typo** — `lab.commission-post-xguild` called `nsg writ-show` (no such command) instead of `nsg writ show`. Earlier phases ran `waitForTerminal=false` and never exercised this path.

## What's left

- **Phase 2c** — baseline vs strong-prompt A/B with N runs per variant. Now unblocked.
- **Open follow-ups** (filed but not addressed this session): the spider ordering bug, the probe-git-range diff-stat bug (`c-mono8...` filed under `c-monew2rg`), plus the three pre-existing parked clicks (`c-monewa82` rigTemplate null, `c-momm4abc` orphan cleanup, `c-momm4i15` daemon-CLI drift).

Apparatus pipeline now end-to-end-validated against a live sonnet implementer + reviewer + revise + seal. The Laboratory works.

content: Continuing the X016 phase-2b handoff: first implementer-driven Laboratory trial completed successfully (~2.5 min, $0.28, 1 codex commit, 3 sessions captured). Next action is phase 2c, the baseline vs strong-prompt A/B comparison. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: was that a test to actually find anything out? or just excercise things?

content.text:
Just an exercise. That trial was the apparatus's first end-to-end stress test — the goal was "does the pipeline run cleanly from codex-setup through teardown when a real implementer is in the loop," not "what does this datapoint tell us about orientation tax."

Concretely:

- **Only one variant ran** (the baseline brief), with no strong-prompt counterpart. You can't measure the suppression effect from one side of an A/B.
- **N=1.** Even if there were a counterpart, one run per variant is noise, not signal.
- **The brief itself is too small** to exhibit orientation tax. multiplySafely is so trivial that there's nothing to "orient" against — the implementer doesn't need to grep a codebase, re-read prior plans, or re-validate handoff state. Orientation tax is interesting at the seam between a fresh session and a non-trivial mid-flow context. This brief gives a fresh session and a *trivial* fully-specified context.
- **No fresh-session-mid-flow setup.** The X016 hypothesis specifically targets the cost of entering a session that's already mid-task (per the X010 H1 piece-session result). Phase 2b just ran a single greenfield implementer session — no prior session, no handoff, no continuity-gap to pay tax on.

So the data captured (turn counts, token splits, cost) is real but not pointed at the research question. It's a **calibration run** — proof that next time we post a trial that actually tests H1, the apparatus will faithfully record what happened.

Phase 2c is where the actual experiment lives. That'll need: the strong-prompt brief variant, a non-trivial brief that creates real orientation surface, a way to simulate a mid-flow handoff (or pick a brief whose first turn naturally requires orientation), and N runs per variant for statistical traction.

# Handoff — Laboratory phase 2c + small follow-ups

The Laboratory apparatus is **end-to-end-validated.** Phase 2b's
first implementer-driven trial ran 16/16 engines green against a
live sonnet implementer + reviewer + revise + seal pipeline; the
test guild's daemon spun up, dispatched a real rig, captured 3
animator sessions and 1 codex commit, and tore down cleanly.

This handoff covers what's next: **phase 2c** (the actual
orientation-suppression A/B), the small follow-ups surfaced
during 2b, and the lingering vibers operational issues.

## Where we are

```
6666dbee X016 phase 2b trial 1: first successful implementer-driven Laboratory trial   ← latest
77c473b7 laboratory: fix writ-show typo + work around spider ordering bug for X016 2b
ce302ef4 X016 phase 2b: brief + manifest for first implementer-driven trial
ba548127 session-notes(lab-impl-2): wrap-up
e3cff686 X016 phase 2a — first daemon-fixture trial completed end-to-end
```

228/228 unit tests passing; typecheck clean; build clean.

## Phase 2b — what landed

**The trial:** `w-monnnuqw-c998a09d0e9f`, rig
`rig-monnny9h-786e0be8`, archive `lar-monnr5zx-7868fa26482d`.
Wallclock **2 min 31 s**, total cost **$0.278** — matched the
$0.30 pre-trial budget with no debug-round overhead because
both bugs surfaced at the apparatus layer (no API calls wasted
on retries).

**Three real animator sessions captured:**

- `implement` (artificer/sonnet): 102.8 s, $0.207, 15in / 5527out
  / 245k cache-read / 13k cache-write
- `review` (reviewer): 11.4 s, $0.045, 3in / 380out / 11.5k
  cache-read / 8.8k cache-write — no rejection
- `revise` (artificer): 8.1 s, $0.026, 4in / 324out / 24.7k
  cache-read / 3.2k cache-write

**One codex commit captured:**
`feat(nexus-core): add multiplySafely utility` at SHA
`7b984d611010`, 1562 bytes diff (numeric.ts + numeric.test.ts
both new files in `packages/framework/core/src/util/`). Function
shape matches the brief precisely.

**Two real bugs caught and fixed:**

1. **Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb** (`c-monniwt3`).
   `spider.start()` calls `g.apparatus('animator')` directly but
   only declares `requires: ['stacks','clerk','fabricator']`.
   Topo sort starts spider before animator unless animator
   appears first in `guild.json plugins`. Vibers hides this
   (animator at position 0, spider at 14). Worked around in
   `baseline-execution-2b.yaml` by ordering [animator, loom,
   claude-code] before [spider, clockworks].

2. **`writ-show` typo in `lab.commission-post-xguild`** (fixed
   in scenario-xguild.ts:236-244). The poll loop called
   `nsg writ-show` (no such top-level command in 0.1.292) instead
   of `nsg writ show` (sub-subcommand). Earlier phases all ran
   `waitForTerminal=false` and never exercised this path.

**Important honest note:** phase 2b was a calibration run, not
a research finding. Only one variant ran (no strong-prompt A
counterpart), N=1, and the brief (multiplySafely) is too small
to exhibit orientation tax. The data captured proves the
apparatus faithfully records what happened — it does NOT
answer the X016 research question. **Phase 2c is where the
actual experiment lives.**

## What's next — main work

### Phase 2c — the actual orientation-suppression A/B (`c-monrvyj9`)

This is no longer "post a trial and see what happens." Phase 2c
needs an explicit design pass before any trial gets posted.
Open questions enumerated in the click; the headline ones:

1. **Strong-prompt placement.** Does the imperative ("Begin
   work immediately. Do not summarize. First turn = code change.")
   go at the top of the brief, in a trailing instruction block,
   or replicated in the `artificer` role definition? Each gives
   different prominence and persistence across handoff.
2. **Brief size and shape.** multiplySafely has no orientation
   surface — the spec is so complete the implementer doesn't
   need to grep, re-read prior plans, or re-validate handoff
   state. Phase 2c needs a brief that creates real orientation
   work: multi-file change, cross-package, or an explicit
   mid-task handoff.
3. **Mid-flow simulation.** H1 specifically targets fresh
   sessions entering AFTER a handoff. Either run two-stage
   trials (session 1 does part, fresh session 2 picks up via
   a handoff note) or pick a brief whose first turn naturally
   requires orientation. The first option exercises the
   actual hypothesis but costs 2× per data point; the second
   is cheaper but tests a weaker version.
4. **N per variant.** Calibration is $0.28/run. N=10 per variant
   (baseline + strong) = $5.60 total. N=20 = $11. Probably want
   N=20 minimum for any statistical traction given the noise
   floor.
5. **Outcome metric.** "Turns to first productive edit" needs
   a precise definition (first commit? first non-readonly tool
   call? first Edit/Write?). The probe doesn't currently slice
   transcripts that way; need a probe extension or a sanctum-
   side analysis pass after `lab.probe-stacks-dump` captures
   `animator/transcripts`.
6. **Quality control.** Same brief, multiple runs — how do we
   ensure noise (a single lucky/unlucky run) doesn't dominate?
   Randomize-and-bracket strategy worth thinking through.

**Pre-flight blocker:** fix `lab.probe-git-range`'s diff-stat
bug (`c-monnvxkz`) before posting phase-2c trials so we have
usable summary stats for the A/B comparison. Cosmetic but
distorts every comparison.

Sean's directives from this conversation:
- Phase 2b was a calibration exercise, not a research finding.
  Phase 2c needs explicit design work before posting trials.
- Open clicks for follow-ups (done — `c-monrvyj9` for 2c design,
  `c-monrw51a` for daemon disappearance, plus the two from
  mid-2b: `c-monniwt3` spider ordering, `c-monnvxkz` diff-stats).

### Small follow-ups surfaced during 2b

**`c-monniwt3` — Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb.** Real
framework bug. Three fix options enumerated; preferred is to
add `'animator'` to spider's `requires`. Tail line in the
click flags that the same pattern probably exists in other
apparatuses (any `start()` calling `g.apparatus(X)` without
`X` in `requires` is a latent ordering bomb) — worth a sweep
across `packages/plugins/` while fixing spider. Tight, ~30-60
min shaped (single-line fix + audit).

**`c-monnvxkz` — `lab.probe-git-range` diff-stats are all
zero.** commits-manifest.yaml writes
`{filesChanged: 0, insertions: 0, deletions: 0}` per commit
even when the patch has content. Probe is likely either
mis-parsing `git log --numstat`, running git in the wrong cwd,
or writing the manifest before computing stats. ~30 min
triage; cosmetic for the apparatus but distorts summary stats
for phase 2c. **Worth fixing before phase 2c.**

**`c-monrw51a` — Vibers daemon disappeared between sessions.**
At lab-impl-3 session start, no pidfile and no `nsg start`
process. Restart was clean. No obvious cause; container
restart and OOM-kill are the most likely candidates. Tight
observation click — needs recurrence to escalate. Cosmetic
for now (manual restart took 5 seconds), but if it keeps
happening, autonomous operation will break the moment the
patron isn't around to restart.

### Pre-existing parked clicks (still relevant)

```
c-monewa82  rigTemplate null in probe summary       — confirmed still real
c-momm4abc  Failed-trial orphan cleanup              — surfaced again in 2b debug loop
c-momm4i15  Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium drift           — `-373f8f51` orphan dir
                                                       reappeared after rm-rf
                                                       because vibers' codex
                                                       registry still has it
```

None are gating; all could be palette-cleansers if phase 2c
design feels too heavy.

## Vibers operational issues observed during 2b

Pre-existing, not phase-2 caused, but worth a janitor pass at
some point:

1. **Daemon dies between sessions.** New observation this
   session — see `c-monrw51a`. Watch for recurrence.
2. **Recurring DB-locked errors.** Same as last handoff —
   `ses-modnht6d` zombie session, vibers' DB (1.7GB) hot path
   contention. Transient; doesn't block.
3. **Three orphan codexes still in registry.**
   `verify-fixtures-{1267530e, 2244ce82, 40dd2663}` per
   `c-momm4i15`. Cosmetic spam in daemon.err.
4. **One new orphan test-guild dir.**
   `/workspace/vibers/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/x016-baseline-2b-implementer-373f8f51`
   from the second failed trial attempt this session. It got
   rm-rf'd but was recreated by vibers' daemon trying to access
   the still-registered codex on next restart. Same root cause
   as `c-momm4i15`.

## Pointers

- **Engine source (writ-show fix)**: `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts:236-244`
- **Engine source (daemon fixture)**: `packages/laboratory/src/engines/daemon-fixture.ts`
- **Phase-2b manifest**: `experiments/X016-orientation-suppression/manifests/baseline-execution-2b.yaml`

  - `README.md` — auto-generated trial summary

  - `codex-history/0000-7b984d611010.patch` — the implementer's commit
  - `trial-context.yaml` — full manifest snapshot + lab-host metadata
- **X016 spec**: `experiments/X016-orientation-suppression/spec.md`
- **Spider's plugin-default template** (still relevant for any

- **Spider's apparatus-startup bug site** (for fixing

  (where start() calls `g.apparatus('animator')`)
- **probe-git-range source** (for fixing `c-monnvxkz`): grep

## Active clicks at handoff

            (engines built; 2a + 2b validated; 2c is where the experiment runs)

first implementer-driven trial ran 16/16 engines green against a
live sonnet implementer + reviewer + revise + seal pipeline; the
test guild's daemon spun up, dispatched a real rig, captured 3
animator sessions and 1 codex commit, and tore down cleanly.

This handoff covers what's next: **phase 2c** (the actual
orientation-suppression A/B), the small follow-ups surfaced
during 2b, and the lingering vibers operational issues.

## Where we are

```

77c473b7 laboratory: fix writ-show typo + work around spider ordering bug for X016 2b
ce302ef4 X016 phase 2b: brief + manifest for first implementer-driven trial
ba548127 session-notes(lab-impl-2): wrap-up
e3cff686 X016 phase 2a — first daemon-fixture trial completed end-to-end
```

228/228 unit tests passing; typecheck clean; build clean.

## Phase 2b — what landed

**The trial:** `w-monnnuqw-c998a09d0e9f`, rig
`rig-monnny9h-786e0be8`, archive `lar-monnr5zx-7868fa26482d`.
Wallclock **2 min 31 s**, total cost **$0.278** — matched the
$0.30 pre-trial budget with no debug-round overhead because
both bugs surfaced at the apparatus layer (no API calls wasted
on retries).

**Three real animator sessions captured:**

- `implement` (artificer/sonnet): 102.8 s, $0.207, 15in / 5527out
  / 245k cache-read / 13k cache-write
- `review` (reviewer): 11.4 s, $0.045, 3in / 380out / 11.5k
  cache-read / 8.8k cache-write — no rejection
- `revise` (artificer): 8.1 s, $0.026, 4in / 324out / 24.7k
  cache-read / 3.2k cache-write

**One codex commit captured:**
`feat(nexus-core): add multiplySafely utility` at SHA
`7b984d611010`, 1562 bytes diff (numeric.ts + numeric.test.ts
both new files in `packages/framework/core/src/util/`). Function
shape matches the brief precisely.

**Two real bugs caught and fixed:**

1. **Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb** (`c-monniwt3`).
   `spider.start()` calls `g.apparatus('animator')` directly but
   only declares `requires: ['stacks','clerk','fabricator']`.
   Topo sort starts spider before animator unless animator
   appears first in `guild.json plugins`. Vibers hides this
   (animator at position 0, spider at 14). Worked around in
   `baseline-execution-2b.yaml` by ordering [animator, loom,
   claude-code] before [spider, clockworks].

2. **`writ-show` typo in `lab.commission-post-xguild`** (fixed
   in scenario-xguild.ts:236-244). The poll loop called
   `nsg writ-show` (no such top-level command in 0.1.292) instead
   of `nsg writ show` (sub-subcommand). Earlier phases all ran
   `waitForTerminal=false` and never exercised this path.

**Important honest note:** phase 2b was a calibration run, not
a research finding. Only one variant ran (no strong-prompt A
counterpart), N=1, and the brief (multiplySafely) is too small
to exhibit orientation tax. The data captured proves the
apparatus faithfully records what happened — it does NOT
answer the X016 research question. **Phase 2c is where the
actual experiment lives.**

## What's next — main work

### Phase 2c — the actual orientation-suppression A/B (`c-monrvyj9`)

This is no longer "post a trial and see what happens." Phase 2c
needs an explicit design pass before any trial gets posted.
Open questions enumerated in the click; the headline ones:

1. **Strong-prompt placement.** Does the imperative ("Begin
   work immediately. Do not summarize. First turn = code change.")
   go at the top of the brief, in a trailing instruction block,
   or replicated in the `artificer` role definition? Each gives
   different prominence and persistence across handoff.
2. **Brief size and shape.** multiplySafely has no orientation
   surface — the spec is so complete the implementer doesn't
   need to grep, re-read prior plans, or re-validate handoff
   state. Phase 2c needs a brief that creates real orientation
   work: multi-file change, cross-package, or an explicit
   mid-task handoff.
3. **Mid-flow simulation.** H1 specifically targets fresh
   sessions entering AFTER a handoff. Either run two-stage
   trials (session 1 does part, fresh session 2 picks up via
   a handoff note) or pick a brief whose first turn naturally
   requires orientation. The first option exercises the
   actual hypothesis but costs 2× per data point; the second
   is cheaper but tests a weaker version.
4. **N per variant.** Calibration is $0.28/run. N=10 per variant
   (baseline + strong) = $5.60 total. N=20 = $11. Probably want
   N=20 minimum for any statistical traction given the noise
   floor.
5. **Outcome metric.** "Turns to first productive edit" needs
   a precise definition (first commit? first non-readonly tool
   call? first Edit/Write?). The probe doesn't currently slice
   transcripts that way; need a probe extension or a sanctum-
   side analysis pass after `lab.probe-stacks-dump` captures
   `animator/transcripts`.
6. **Quality control.** Same brief, multiple runs — how do we
   ensure noise (a single lucky/unlucky run) doesn't dominate?
   Randomize-and-bracket strategy worth thinking through.

**Pre-flight blocker:** fix `lab.probe-git-range`'s diff-stat
bug (`c-monnvxkz`) before posting phase-2c trials so we have
usable summary stats for the A/B comparison. Cosmetic but
distorts every comparison.

Sean's directives from this conversation:
- Phase 2b was a calibration exercise, not a research finding.
  Phase 2c needs explicit design work before posting trials.
- Open clicks for follow-ups (done — `c-monrvyj9` for 2c design,
  `c-monrw51a` for daemon disappearance, plus the two from
  mid-2b: `c-monniwt3` spider ordering, `c-monnvxkz` diff-stats).

### Small follow-ups surfaced during 2b

**`c-monniwt3` — Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb.** Real
framework bug. Three fix options enumerated; preferred is to
add `'animator'` to spider's `requires`. Tail line in the
click flags that the same pattern probably exists in other
apparatuses (any `start()` calling `g.apparatus(X)` without
`X` in `requires` is a latent ordering bomb) — worth a sweep
across `packages/plugins/` while fixing spider. Tight, ~30-60
min shaped (single-line fix + audit).

**`c-monnvxkz` — `lab.probe-git-range` diff-stats are all
zero.** commits-manifest.yaml writes
`{filesChanged: 0, insertions: 0, deletions: 0}` per commit
even when the patch has content. Probe is likely either
mis-parsing `git log --numstat`, running git in the wrong cwd,
or writing the manifest before computing stats. ~30 min
triage; cosmetic for the apparatus but distorts summary stats
for phase 2c. **Worth fixing before phase 2c.**

**`c-monrw51a` — Vibers daemon disappeared between sessions.**
At lab-impl-3 session start, no pidfile and no `nsg start`
process. Restart was clean. No obvious cause; container
restart and OOM-kill are the most likely candidates. Tight
observation click — needs recurrence to escalate. Cosmetic
for now (manual restart took 5 seconds), but if it keeps
happening, autonomous operation will break the moment the
patron isn't around to restart.

### Pre-existing parked clicks (still relevant)

```
c-monewa82  rigTemplate null in probe summary       — confirmed still real
c-momm4abc  Failed-trial orphan cleanup              — surfaced again in 2b debug loop
c-momm4i15  Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium drift           — `-373f8f51` orphan dir
                                                       reappeared after rm-rf
                                                       because vibers' codex
                                                       registry still has it
```

None are gating; all could be palette-cleansers if phase 2c
design feels too heavy.

## Vibers operational issues observed during 2b

Pre-existing, not phase-2 caused, but worth a janitor pass at
some point:

1. **Daemon dies between sessions.** New observation this
   session — see `c-monrw51a`. Watch for recurrence.
2. **Recurring DB-locked errors.** Same as last handoff —
   `ses-modnht6d` zombie session, vibers' DB (1.7GB) hot path
   contention. Transient; doesn't block.
3. **Three orphan codexes still in registry.**
   `verify-fixtures-{1267530e, 2244ce82, 40dd2663}` per
   `c-momm4i15`. Cosmetic spam in daemon.err.
4. **One new orphan test-guild dir.**
   `/workspace/vibers/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/x016-baseline-2b-implementer-373f8f51`
   from the second failed trial attempt this session. It got
   rm-rf'd but was recreated by vibers' daemon trying to access
   the still-registered codex on next restart. Same root cause
   as `c-momm4i15`.

## Pointers

- **Engine source (writ-show fix)**: `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts:236-244`
- **Engine source (daemon fixture)**: `packages/laboratory/src/engines/daemon-fixture.ts`
- **Phase-2b manifest**: `experiments/X016-orientation-suppression/manifests/baseline-execution-2b.yaml`
- **Phase-2b artifacts**: `experiments/X016-orientation-suppression/artifacts/2026-05-02-baseline-2b-implementer/`
  - `README.md` — auto-generated trial summary
  - `stacks-export/animator-sessions.json` — the three sessions with full token + cost data
  - `codex-history/0000-7b984d611010.patch` — the implementer's commit
  - `trial-context.yaml` — full manifest snapshot + lab-host metadata
- **X016 spec**: `experiments/X016-orientation-suppression/spec.md`
- **Spider's plugin-default template** (still relevant for any
  future trial composing rigs): `/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/default-template.ts`
- **Spider's apparatus-startup bug site** (for fixing
  `c-monniwt3`): `/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider.ts:3164-3175`
  (where start() calls `g.apparatus('animator')`)
- **probe-git-range source** (for fixing `c-monnvxkz`): grep
  `packages/laboratory/src` for `commits-manifest`

## Active clicks at handoff

```
c-monew2rg  X016 phase 2 — daemon lifecycle + full implementer execution      [LIVE]
            (engines built; 2a + 2b validated; 2c is where the experiment runs)
├── c-monrvyj9  Phase 2c — design + run the actual orientation A/B            [LIVE]
├── c-monniwt3  Spider 0.1.292 apparatus.start() ordering bomb                [LIVE]
└── c-monnvxkz  lab.probe-git-range diff-stats are all zero                   [LIVE]

```

`c-monew2rg`'s goal text covers the broader phase-2 scope
through the experiment itself; the click stays live until
phase 2c produces actual orientation-suppression data.

## Live environment state

- **Vibers daemon**: running (pid 1902978, restarted at lab-impl-3
  start). Standard ports 7470/7471. Three known cosmetic orphan
  codexes still in registry; new orphan test-guild dir
  `x016-baseline-2b-implementer-373f8f51` left behind from the
  second-attempt trial in this session (rm-rf'd, recreated by
  daemon, no functional impact).
- **Phase-2b test guild + codex**: cleanly torn down by trial
  3 — only the `-373f8f51` orphan remains (from trial 2 failure).
- **All commits land on `main`** in `nexus-mk2`. The `nexus`
  repo has 2 commits ahead of origin/main from a prior session;
  nothing pushed (per the no-push-without-explicit-ask
  directive).
- **Authentication**: vibers has working `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in
  its environment. Phase 2c trials will inherit through `nsg
  start`'s child process chain (verified in 2b — sonnet
  implementer ran cleanly).

## Tone note

Phase 2c is the natural next big piece, but **it's a design
pass first, not a "post a trial" task.** The apparatus is
proven; the experiment isn't designed yet. Pick it up when
you have time + budget for thinking through the open questions
listed under `c-monrvyj9` (and time + ~$10-20 of API budget
when actual trials start).

If phase 2c design feels too heavy, **`c-monnvxkz`** (probe
diff-stats) is a tight palette-cleanser — pre-flight blocker
for 2c anyway, ~30 min, no API cost. **`c-monniwt3`** (spider
ordering) is also tight if you want to pay down framework debt
while you're in there — single-line fix to spider + a quick
audit sweep across other plugins.

Base directory for this skill: /workspace/nexus-mk2/.claude/skills/wrap-up

# Wrap-Up: Complete Work and Generate Session Summary

## Distill the session (run first)

Before generating the ethnographer summary, distill the session into a
durable planning artifact. Invoke:

    /workspace/nexus-mk2/bin/coco-distill.sh <session-id>

Where `<session-id>` is your cached Claude session ID (resolved at
startup). The script:

1. Runs the **distiller** agent against the session's JSONL transcript and
   writes a structured distill to `docs/planning/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>.md`
   (slug chosen by the distiller from the conversation focus).
2. Runs the **verifier** agent against the distill + transcript. The
   verifier is calibrated for a high severity bar — it stays silent
   except for genuinely serious discrepancies.

If the verifier prints findings, surface them to Sean and offer to revise
the distill before continuing. If the verifier is silent (the expected
case), the distill is good as written.

The distill lives in git and is the canonical planning record of the
session. Review it briefly before the ethnographer summary — the
ethnographer summary should not duplicate the distill but can reference
it.

## Generate Summary

Generate a structured summary of the current session. This summary serves one audience:

**The Scribe** — as input for session notes and published material.

<expected-format>
# Session Summary

## What we did

- Bulleted list of concrete actions taken (files created/modified, decisions made, commits pushed). Reference specific files and commit hashes.

## Decisions made and/or deferred

- What was decided, including explicit "we chose NOT to" decisions. Brief rationale for each.

## Next steps & open questions

- Concrete actions for the next session or for agents to pick up.
- Open questions which were raised but not answered

### Notable moments

Capture quotes, insights, or exchanges that stand out — either for system design or for the research/experiment narrative.

For each notable moment:
- Include **direct quotes from Sean** when available
- Flag **why it's notable** (e.g., "shifted the project's core abstraction", "surfaced a tension between X and Y", "revealed an assumption about how agents should work")
- Prioritize moments where the human changed direction, challenged an assumption, or articulated something new about the project's purpose

### Experiment observations

Flag anything relevant to active experiments. Omit this section if nothing applies.

<expected-format>

## Complete Work

After wrapping up, **ALWAYS**:

- Commit any changes you have made, if any
- Push main to the remote

## Reminders

Check for things Sean should be nudged about:

- **Ethnographer interview:** Check the most recent interview file in `experiments/ethnography/interviews/` (files named `YYYY-MM-DDTHHMMSS.md`). If the most recent one is more than a day old — or if none exist yet — remind Sean to run an ethnographer session. Keep it light: *"It's been a couple days since your last ethnographer interview — might be a good time to check in."*

## Guidelines

- Keep the whole summary under ~500 words. Brevity is a feature.
- Do not editorialize beyond the "why it's notable" flags.
- Write the summary to `experiments/ethnography/session-notes/new/<timestamp>.md`. where `<timestamp>` is the current date and time formatted as `YYYY-MM-DDTHHMMSS.md` (use the actual current time — don't round to the nearest hour). Each session gets its own file — never overwrite a previous one. This file is for the ethnographer and must NOT be auto-deleted — it accumulates until the ethnographer reviews it.
- Commit all `session-notes`, `docs/planning/` (newly written distill), and `experiments/data/transcripts/` changes to git and push those changes. The transcripts directory may contain files from concurrent sessions — commit whatever is there.
- In addition to writing the summary, share it in the chat for review and inclusion in session transcripts
Running the distill first.

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# coco-distill.sh — Distill a Coco session transcript into docs/planning/
# and run a verifier; print verifier findings only on discrepancy.
#
# Usage: coco-distill.sh <session-id>
#
# Output: writes docs/planning/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>.md (slug chosen by the
# distiller agent based on the conversation focus). Prints the path on
# success. If the verifier finds serious discrepancies, prints the
# findings to stdout for Sean's review; otherwise silent.

set -euo pipefail

SESSION_ID="${1:?usage: $(basename "$0") <session-id>}"
SANCTUM="/workspace/nexus-mk2"
TRANSCRIPT="$SANCTUM/experiments/data/transcripts/$SESSION_ID.jsonl"
PLANNING_DIR="$SANCTUM/docs/planning"

if [[ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT" ]]; then
    echo "transcript not found: $TRANSCRIPT" >&2
    exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$PLANNING_DIR"

# 1. Run the distiller agent.
DISTILL_RAW="$(claude --add-dir "$SANCTUM" --allowedTools Read --agent distiller \
    -p "Distill the transcript at $TRANSCRIPT" --output-format text)"

# 1a. Strip anything before the first '---' line — agents sometimes leak a

input.content:
# Session Summary

Session: `7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37` (lab-impl-3)
Date: 2026-05-02

## What we did

- Authored brief + manifest for X016 phase 2b — first
  implementer-driven Laboratory trial. Brief retargeted at
  `packages/framework/core/` (smallest, zero-dep framework
  package); plugin set extended with animator + loom +
  claude-code at 0.1.292; deep-merged loom/animator/spider
  config into the test guild via `lab.guild-setup`'s `config`
  given. (Commit `ce302ef4`.)
- First attempt (`w-monn5qa9`) failed — surfaced **Spider
  0.1.292 ordering bomb**: `spider.start()` calls
  `g.apparatus('animator')` but only declares
  `requires=['stacks','clerk','fabricator']`. Vibers hides this
  by accident (animator at position 0 in the plugin list,
  spider at 14). Filed as `c-monniwt3` with three fix options.
- Second attempt (`w-monnj0vq`) failed — surfaced
  **`writ-show` typo** in `lab.commission-post-xguild`'s
  poll loop; fixed in `scenario-xguild.ts` (should be `nsg
  writ show` not `nsg writ-show`). Earlier phases all ran
  `waitForTerminal=false` and never exercised the path.
  (Commit `77c473b7`.)
- Third attempt (`w-monnnuqw`) **completed end-to-end** in
  2m 31s, $0.278 total. Three real animator sessions
  captured (implement 102.8s/$0.207, review 11.4s/$0.045,
  revise 8.1s/$0.026); 1 codex commit captured
  (`feat(nexus-core): add multiplySafely utility`, 1562
  bytes diff). Apparatus end-to-end-validated. Updated spec
  status log, experiment index, extracted artifacts.
  (Commit `6666dbee`.)
- Filed two more clicks during wrap-up: `c-monrvyj9`
  (phase 2c design — six open questions enumerated) and
  `c-monrw51a` (vibers daemon disappeared between sessions,
  observation only).
- Wrote handoff at `.scratch/handoff-lab-phase-2c.md`.

## Decisions made and/or deferred

- **Skip astrolabe in the test guild.** Verified Spider's
  plugin-default `mandate → default` fallback fires
  automatically when no kit claims `mandate`; no explicit
  mapping needed. Keeps the implementer measurement clean
  of planner overhead.
- **Target `framework/core` for the brief.** Smallest,
  zero-dep package; build/test commands package-scoped via
  `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core` to avoid
  recursive-pnpm purgatory.
- **Sibling brief file** (Sean's call: "just go siblings").
  New `briefs/baseline-task-core.md` rather than rewriting
  the existing `baseline-task.md`, so phase-1/2a manifests
  stay reproducible.
- **Phase 2b is a calibration run, not a research finding.**
  Sean called this out explicitly. Phase 2c is where the
  actual experiment lives — needs explicit design pass before
  posting trials.
- **Worked around the Spider ordering bug rather than fixing
  it.** Reordered the test guild's plugin list so animator
  precedes spider. Fix click filed for proper resolution
  later.

## Next steps & open questions

- **Phase 2c design** (`c-monrvyj9`) — strong-prompt
  placement, brief size and shape, mid-flow simulation, N
  per variant, outcome metric definition, quality control.
  Pre-flight blocker: fix `c-monnvxkz` (probe diff-stats)
  so summary stats are usable.
- Two palette-cleansers if 2c design feels heavy:
  `c-monnvxkz` (probe-git-range diff-stats, ~30 min, no API
  cost, pre-flight blocker for 2c) and `c-monniwt3` (spider
  ordering, single-line fix + audit sweep across plugins).
- Watch `c-monrw51a` for recurrence — vibers daemon
  disappeared between this session and the prior one with
  no obvious cause.

### Notable moments

- **Sean's reality check on the trial:** *"was that a test
  to actually find anything out? or just exercise things?"* —
  Asked after I'd written up the trial as a success story.
  Notable because it forced an honest distinction between
  "the apparatus works" (true) and "we learned something
  about orientation tax" (false). I'd been describing phase
  2b as if it answered the X016 question; Sean's question
  surfaced that one variant + N=1 + a brief with no
  orientation surface = calibration data, not signal. The
  follow-up summary made the calibration-vs-finding
  distinction explicit and reframed phase 2c as the real
  experiment.
- **Sean on the sibling brief decision:** *"just go
  siblings"* — One-line resolution to a question I'd
  flagged. Notable for the conciseness; not every decision
  needs a paragraph.

### Experiment observations

**X016 (Orientation Suppression):** Apparatus
end-to-end-validated. First real implementer-driven trial
ran 16/16 engines green at $0.278/2.5min. The captured
data is reliable — three animator sessions with full
token splits, codex commit with real patch content. But
the trial was a calibration run, not a research finding;
the brief was too trivial to exhibit orientation tax and
N=1 with one variant is noise. Phase 2c design (the
actual A/B with N runs per variant on a brief with real
orientation surface) is the next step. Cost calibration:
$0.28/run at sonnet for a small task; phase 2c with N=20
per variant ≈ $11.

**Laboratory apparatus:** Two real bugs surfaced and fixed
during the trial loop — both at the apparatus layer (no
API calls wasted on retries). Spider ordering bomb is a
real framework bug but worked around in the manifest.
`writ-show` typo was a Laboratory bug, fixed in source.
228/228 unit tests still passing post-fix.

73
mkdir -p "$PLANNING_DIR"

# 1. Run the distiller agent.
DISTILL_RAW="$(claude --add-dir "$SANCTUM" --allowedTools Read --agent distiller \
    -p "Distill the transcript at $TRANSCRIPT" --output-format text)"

# 1a. Strip anything before the first '---' line — agents sometimes leak a
# preamble or commentary before the frontmatter despite instructions.
DISTILL="$(printf '%s\n' "$DISTILL_RAW" | awk '/^---$/{found=1} found{print}')"

if [[ -z "$DISTILL" ]]; then
    echo "distiller produced no frontmatter-prefixed output" >&2
    echo "raw output:" >&2
    printf '%s\n' "$DISTILL_RAW" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# 2. Extract slug and date from the YAML frontmatter the distiller emits.
SLUG="$(printf '%s\n' "$DISTILL" | awk '/^slug: /{print $2; exit}')"
DATE="$(printf '%s\n' "$DISTILL" | awk '/^date: /{print $2; exit}')"

if [[ -z "$SLUG" || -z "$DATE" ]]; then
    echo "distiller did not emit valid frontmatter (slug=$SLUG date=$DATE)" >&2
    echo "raw output:" >&2
    printf '%s\n' "$DISTILL" >&2
    exit 1
fi

DISTILL_PATH="$PLANNING_DIR/$DATE-$SLUG.md"

# If a file already exists for this date+slug, append a numeric suffix.
n=2
while [[ -e "$DISTILL_PATH" ]]; do
    DISTILL_PATH="$PLANNING_DIR/$DATE-$SLUG-$n.md"
    ((n++))
done

printf '%s\n' "$DISTILL" > "$DISTILL_PATH"
echo "distill: $DISTILL_PATH"

# 3. Run the verifier agent against the distill + transcript.
VERIFY="$(claude --add-dir "$SANCTUM" --allowedTools Read --agent verifier \
    -p "Verify the distill at $DISTILL_PATH against the transcript at $TRANSCRIPT" \
    --output-format text)"

# 4. Surface verifier output only if it reports a discrepancy.
if printf '%s\n' "$VERIFY" | head -1 | grep -q '^STATUS: discrepancy'; then
    echo ""
    printf '%s\n' "$VERIFY" | tail -n +2
fi

2	slug: x016-phase-2b-implementer-trial
3	date: 2026-05-02
4	session: 7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37
5	---
6	
7	# Session distill — X016 phase 2b first implementer trial
8	
9	## Intent — what Sean asked for
10	- Continue phase 2b work from `.scratch/handoff-lab-phase-2b.md`: resolve 6 open design questions, author a brief and manifest targeting the nexus monorepo, and get a real implementer session to run end-to-end in a Laboratory test guild (msg 1).
11	- Brief file strategy: add a sibling `baseline-task-core.md` targeting `@shardworks/nexus-core`, leave the original `baseline-task.md` intact so prior trial manifests stay reproducible (msg 3 — "just go siblings").
12	- After trial succeeded: open clicks for all identified follow-up items, produce a handoff prompt for next session (msg 7).
13	
14	## In-flight inquiries
15	
16	### I1 — Phase 2c design
17	**Question**: How to structure the orientation-suppression A/B experiment proper (baseline vs strong-prompt variants, N runs, outcome metric, QC).
18	**What we've considered**: Six open questions enumerated in click `c-monrvyj9`: where in the brief the strong-prompt material goes, how big the brief should be to stress orientation, whether to simulate mid-flow context loss, how many runs per variant, what metric captures orientation tax, and QC thresholds for keeping/discarding sessions.
19	**What we've ruled out (this session)**: Nothing yet ruled out — purely enumeration.
20	**Where we got stuck / what we need next**: Phase 2c is a design pass first, not a "post a trial" task. Need answers to those six questions before authoring the strong-prompt brief variant. Pre-flight blocker: `lab.probe-git-range` reports `filesChanged/insertions/deletions = 0` for every commit (click `c-monnvxkz`) — must be fixed before phase 2c so the commit-level stats are usable in the A/B comparison.
21	
22	## Decisions
23	
24	### D1 — Skip astrolabe; rely on Spider's mandate→default builtin fallback
25	**Status**: accepted
26	**Context**: Handoff listed two paths for getting a mandate writ dispatched to the default rig template — install astrolabe (gets the mapping for free but adds planner overhead) or configure `rigTemplateMappings` manually. A third option surfaced from reading `spider.ts:1389-1397` (msg 2).
27	**Decision**: No astrolabe and no explicit `rigTemplateMappings`. Spider's builtin fallback fires automatically when no kit claims `mandate` and the `default` template is registered via `supportKit` — both conditions hold without astrolabe. Planner overhead excluded to keep the measurement surface clean.
28	**Consequences**: Test guild plugin list is animator + loom + claude-code on top of phase-2a's seven. No astrolabe, no oculus, no explicit mandate mapping.
29	
30	### D2 — Add sibling brief, leave original alone
31	**Status**: accepted
32	**Context**: `baseline-task.md` targets a top-level `src/util/numeric.ts` path that doesn't exist in the nexus monorepo. Needed to retarget it. Options: rewrite in place or add sibling (msg 2 / msg 3).
33	**Decision**: Sean chose sibling — `baseline-task-core.md` added targeting `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.ts`. Original `baseline-task.md` left intact so phase-1/2a manifest references stay reproducible.
34	**Consequences**: Phase-2b manifest points to `baseline-task-core.md`. The new brief specifies `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test` and `build` as test/build commands.
35	
36	### D3 — Design question resolutions (six open questions)
37	**Status**: accepted
38	**Context**: Handoff listed 6 open questions Coco resolved autonomously before pausing for Sean's brief-file answer (msg 2).
39	**Decision**: (1) No astrolabe, builtin fallback — see D1. (2) `loom.roles.artificer` mirrors vibers exactly (`clerk:* tools:*`), injected via `config` given. (3) Target `@shardworks/nexus-core` — smallest, zero-dep framework package. (4) `spider.variables`: `role=artificer`, `buildCommand=pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core build`, `testCommand=pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test`. (5) `timeoutMs: 900000` (15-min cap). (6) `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` inherits through `process.env` via `execFile` in `lab.daemon-setup` — confirmed no explicit `env:` pass.
40	**Consequences**: `baseline-execution-2b.yaml` encodes all six choices.

---
slug: x016-phase-2b-implementer-trial
date: 2026-05-02
session: 7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37
---

# Session distill — X016 phase 2b first implementer trial

## Intent — what Sean asked for
- Continue phase 2b work from `.scratch/handoff-lab-phase-2b.md`: resolve 6 open design questions, author a brief and manifest targeting the nexus monorepo, and get a real implementer session to run end-to-end in a Laboratory test guild (msg 1).
- Brief file strategy: add a sibling `baseline-task-core.md` targeting `@shardworks/nexus-core`, leave the original `baseline-task.md` intact so prior trial manifests stay reproducible (msg 3 — "just go siblings").
- After trial succeeded: open clicks for all identified follow-up items, produce a handoff prompt for next session (msg 7).

## In-flight inquiries

### I1 — Phase 2c design
**Question**: How to structure the orientation-suppression A/B experiment proper (baseline vs strong-prompt variants, N runs, outcome metric, QC).
**What we've considered**: Six open questions enumerated in click `c-monrvyj9`: where in the brief the strong-prompt material goes, how big the brief should be to stress orientation, whether to simulate mid-flow context loss, how many runs per variant, what metric captures orientation tax, and QC thresholds for keeping/discarding sessions.
**What we've ruled out (this session)**: Nothing yet ruled out — purely enumeration.
**Where we got stuck / what we need next**: Phase 2c is a design pass first, not a "post a trial" task. Need answers to those six questions before authoring the strong-prompt brief variant. Pre-flight blocker: `lab.probe-git-range` reports `filesChanged/insertions/deletions = 0` for every commit (click `c-monnvxkz`) — must be fixed before phase 2c so the commit-level stats are usable in the A/B comparison.

## Decisions

### D1 — Skip astrolabe; rely on Spider's mandate→default builtin fallback
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: Handoff listed two paths for getting a mandate writ dispatched to the default rig template — install astrolabe (gets the mapping for free but adds planner overhead) or configure `rigTemplateMappings` manually. A third option surfaced from reading `spider.ts:1389-1397` (msg 2).
**Decision**: No astrolabe and no explicit `rigTemplateMappings`. Spider's builtin fallback fires automatically when no kit claims `mandate` and the `default` template is registered via `supportKit` — both conditions hold without astrolabe. Planner overhead excluded to keep the measurement surface clean.
**Consequences**: Test guild plugin list is animator + loom + claude-code on top of phase-2a's seven. No astrolabe, no oculus, no explicit mandate mapping.

### D2 — Add sibling brief, leave original alone
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: `baseline-task.md` targets a top-level `src/util/numeric.ts` path that doesn't exist in the nexus monorepo. Needed to retarget it. Options: rewrite in place or add sibling (msg 2 / msg 3).
**Decision**: Sean chose sibling — `baseline-task-core.md` added targeting `packages/framework/core/src/util/numeric.ts`. Original `baseline-task.md` left intact so phase-1/2a manifest references stay reproducible.
**Consequences**: Phase-2b manifest points to `baseline-task-core.md`. The new brief specifies `pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test` and `build` as test/build commands.

### D3 — Design question resolutions (six open questions)
**Status**: accepted
**Context**: Handoff listed 6 open questions Coco resolved autonomously before pausing for Sean's brief-file answer (msg 2).
**Decision**: (1) No astrolabe, builtin fallback — see D1. (2) `loom.roles.artificer` mirrors vibers exactly (`clerk:* tools:*`), injected via `config` given. (3) Target `@shardworks/nexus-core` — smallest, zero-dep framework package. (4) `spider.variables`: `role=artificer`, `buildCommand=pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core build`, `testCommand=pnpm --filter @shardworks/nexus-core test`. (5) `timeoutMs: 900000` (15-min cap). (6) `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` inherits through `process.env` via `execFile` in `lab.daemon-setup` — confirmed no explicit `env:` pass.
**Consequences**: `baseline-execution-2b.yaml` encodes all six choices.

content.text: The distill landed. Now let me commit everything together.

content.content:
# Session Summary

Session: `7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37` (lab-impl-3)
Date: 2026-05-02

## What we did

- Authored brief + manifest for X016 phase 2b — first
  implementer-driven Laboratory trial. Brief retargeted at
  `packages/framework/core/` (smallest, zero-dep framework
  package); plugin set extended with animator + loom +
  claude-code at 0.1.292; deep-merged loom/animator/spider
  config into the test guild via `lab.guild-setup`'s `config`
  given. (Commit `ce302ef4`.)
- First attempt (`w-monn5qa9`) failed — surfaced **Spider
  0.1.292 ordering bomb**: `spider.start()` calls
  `g.apparatus('animator')` but only declares
  `requires=['stacks','clerk','fabricator']`. Vibers hides this
  by accident (animator at position 0 in the plugin list,
  spider at 14). Filed as `c-monniwt3` with three fix options.

  date: 2026-05-02
  item: "Two phase-2c pre-flight bug fixes across nexus + nexus-mk2. (1) lab.probe-git-range diff-stats (c-monnvxkz): readShortStat used 'git show --shortstat --no-patch --format=' which produces empty output — empty --format= swallows the shortstat line. Added 'animator' to requires. Audit sweep across all plugin start() functions found no other ordering-bomb candidates — every plugin either declares its start-time apparatus deps in requires or relies on documented transitive ordering (reckoner uses clerk's transitive 'requires: stacks' with explicit comment). 228/228 laboratory tests + 762/762 spider tests + typechecks all clean."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

  item: "X016 phase 2b — first attempt (w-monn5qa9) and second attempt (w-monnj0vq) both failed; surfaced and fixed two real bugs. (1) Spider 0.1.292 ordering bomb: spider's apparatus.start() calls g.apparatus('animator') but only declares requires=['stacks','clerk','fabricator']. Topo sort starts spider before animator unless animator appears first in the guild.json plugins array. Vibers (animator at position 0, spider at 14) hides this; the X016 test guild had spider before animator and failed loudly with 'apparatus(\"animator\") is not available'. Worked around in baseline-execution-2b.yaml by reordering plugins so [animator, loom, claude-code] precede [spider, clockworks]. Filed click c-monn8wfk under c-monew2rg with three fix options (preferred: add 'animator' to spider's requires) and noted same pattern likely exists in other apparatuses. (2) Real bug in lab.commission-post-xguild's poll loop: it called `nsg writ-show` (no such top-level command in 0.1.292 CLI) instead of `nsg writ show` (sub-subcommand). Earlier phases all ran with waitForTerminal=false and never exercised this path. Fixed in scenario-xguild.ts. 228/228 tests still passing. Trial about to be re-posted with both fixes in place."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

- session: 7198cee2-fdba-4b4d-99f1-2baf6ed73f37
  date: 2026-05-02
  item: "X016 phase 2b — third attempt (w-monnnuqw) completed successfully end-to-end. The Laboratory apparatus is now end-to-end-validated against a live sonnet implementer + reviewer + revise + seal pipeline. 16/16 engines completed in 1 attempt each, ~2.5 minute wallclock. Three real animator sessions captured: implement (102.8s, $0.207), review (11.4s, $0.045), revise (8.1s, $0.026); total $0.278 — matches pre-trial $0.30 budget with no debug-round overhead because both bugs were caught at the apparatus layer (no API calls wasted on retries). 1 codex commit captured: feat(nexus-core): add multiplySafely utility (1562 bytes diff, 2 new files: numeric.ts + numeric.test.ts in packages/framework/core/src/util/). Function shape matches the brief precisely. Verified Spider's plugin-default mandate→default fallback fires automatically without astrolabe. Updated spec status log + experiment index. Filed one open follow-up click for lab.probe-git-range's broken commits-manifest diff-stats (writes filesChanged/insertions/deletions=0 even when patch has content). Phase 2c (baseline vs strong-prompt A/B) is unblocked."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

- session: 3f2392ec-de01-495e-a626-9d1c0f434073
  date: 2026-05-02
  item: "Two phase-2c pre-flight bug fixes across nexus + nexus-mk2. (1) lab.probe-git-range diff-stats (c-monnvxkz): readShortStat used 'git show --shortstat --no-patch --format=' which produces empty output — empty --format= swallows the shortstat line. Switched to 'git log -1 --shortstat --format=' which emits just the shortstat. parseShortStat unchanged; existing unit tests still cover the parser. (2) Spider apparatus ordering bomb (c-monniwt3): spider's start() resolves animator via g.apparatus('animator') but requires only declared ['stacks','clerk','fabricator']. Added 'animator' to requires. Audit sweep across all plugin start() functions found no other ordering-bomb candidates — every plugin either declares its start-time apparatus deps in requires or relies on documented transitive ordering (reckoner uses clerk's transitive 'requires: stacks' with explicit comment). 228/228 laboratory tests + 762/762 spider tests + typechecks all clean."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

content.text:
Pushed. Wrap-up done.

## Answers to your questions

**On the bash spam:** yeah, that was bonkers — I polled the distiller's pid every ~3 seconds for 15 straight minutes. I had a perfectly good background monitor (`bd00jua73`) running an `until ... ; do sleep 60; done` that would notify me on completion, but I kept manually checking anyway. Logged the lesson in the coco-log entry: when a long-running command kicks off, set the until-loop with notification and *drop* the manual polling. Sorry for the noise in your terminal.

**On distill timing:** yes, ~15 minutes. Started at 03:29, finished around 03:44, on a 1.5 MB / 851-line transcript. That's well above typical — the concurrent `lab-impl-2` session filed `c-monrru42` for distiller token cost, noting ~$3.30/cycle on Sonnet 4.5 driven by chunked-Read multi-turn growth on the same scale of transcript. Their fix proposal is jq pre-filter + one-shot prompt for ~6-10× reduction. So the distiller cost/runtime issue is already tracked and queued.

## Quick session-summary recap

Session notes ended up landing in `2026-05-02T033509.md` via the concurrent `lab-impl-2` wrap-up's commit (`5ffc5901`) — they swept my file in along with their own work before my git-status caught it. Distill landed at `docs/planning/2026-05-02-x016-phase-2b-implementer-trial.md` (commit `73013a39`).

**Net session output:**
- 4 commits on `main` (manifest + brief, mid-trial bug fixes, trial success + artifacts, distill)
- 4 clicks filed (`c-monniwt3` spider ordering, `c-monnvxkz` diff-stats, `c-monrvyj9` phase-2c design, `c-monrw51a` daemon disappearance) — first two **already resolved** by parallel session `3f2392ec`
- Phase 2b apparatus end-to-end-validated, $0.278/2.5min for the trial, 1 codex commit + 3 animator sessions captured
- Phase-2c handoff at `.scratch/handoff-lab-phase-2c.md`

**No ethnographer reminder needed** — most recent interview was 2026-05-01, right at the threshold.
